name: DocuSign API
what_it_does: Industry-leading eSignature and agreement management platform API for sending, signing, tracking, and managing legally binding electronic signatures at scale.
use_cases: Automating contract generation and sending for signature from agents
Tracking envelope status and signature completion in workflows
Embedding signing experiences in applications via embedded signing
Managing document templates for repeated contract types
Bulk sending agreements to large numbers of recipients
not_for: Small teams on tight budgets (pricing is steep)
best_when: You need legally binding eSignatures with enterprise-grade audit trails, compliance certifications, and robust agent automation capabilities.
avoid_when: You need simple PDF handling, internal approvals only, or have budget constraints.

auth.notes: OAuth Authorization Code for user-context. JWT (Service Account) flow for server-to-server agent use. JWT is the preferred method for agents - no user interaction required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox: free with full API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Personal $15/mo, Standard $45/mo, Business Pro $65/mo, Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free developer sandbox available for building and testing. Production requires paid subscription. Enterprise pricing can be significant.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 API calls/hour per account on most plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses with errorCode and message. Developer documentation is comprehensive but can be dense.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Agents must track envelope IDs to avoid duplicate sends.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT auth requires RSA private key management - secure key storage is critical for agents
Envelopes in 'sent' status cannot be modified - agents must void and resend if changes needed
Webhook (Connect) configuration requires a publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint with HMAC verification
Sandbox and production use different base URLs and separate accounts
API rate limits are per account across all integrations - multiple agents sharing an account can conflict

security_score_components.security_notes: JWT Service Integration for agent/server auth. OAuth2 for user-facing flows. SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, FedRAMP Moderate. ESIGN/UETA/eIDAS compliant. Complete audit trail per envelope. Sensitive legal documents — access control is critical.
